memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Israel Peck (1644-1723) - mention of Mehitabel
memoryArtifacts.description:
From "A genealogical history of the descendants of Joseph Peck, who emigrated with his family to this country in 1638, and records of his father's and grandfather's families in England, with the pedigree extending back from son to father for twenty generations, with their coat of arms, and copies of wills, also, an appendix, giving an account of the Boston and Hingham Pecks..."
by Peck, Ira Ballou, 1805-1888

Available at: 
https://archive.org/details/genealogicalhist00peck/page/n10

zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Eva de Ufford於1305年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可瑟斯頓時，她的父親Robert de Ufford II26歲，母親Cecily de Valoignes24歲。她和Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr至少有2個兒子。她去世於1370年5月在英國英格蘭素福爾可伍德布里奇享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Eva de Ufford于1305年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可瑟斯頓时，她的父亲Robert de Ufford II26岁，母亲Cecily de Valoignes24岁。她和Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr至少有2个儿子。她去世于1370年5月在英国英格兰素福爾可伍德布里奇享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ufford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ufford (Northamptonshire), Ufford (Suffolk), or Offord Cluny or Offord Darcy (Huntingdonshire). The Northamptonshire and Suffolk placenames derive from the Old English personal name Uffa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’. For the Huntingdonshire placename see Offord , with which the surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Offord
meanings: English (East Anglia and London): habitational name from Offord Cluny and Offord Darcy in Huntindgonshire. The placename derives from Old English upp ‘up, upper’ + ford ‘ford’.
English: alternatively, from a lost place called Offord near Pennyford in Wootton Wawen, Warwickshire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Offa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Eva
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Popular Latinate form of   Eve  , used also as an Anglicization of the Irish name Aoife .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Eva de Ufford was born in 1305, in Thurston, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Ufford II, was 26 and her mother, Cecily de Valoignes, was 24. She had at least 2 sons with Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr. She died in May 1370, in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Eva de Ufford est née en 1305, à Thurston, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Ufford II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Valoignes, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr. Elle est décédée en mai 1370, à Woodbridge, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Eva de Ufford 1305 in Thurston, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Ufford II 26 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Valoignes 24. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb im Mai 1370 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Eva de Ufford è nata il nel 1305 nel Thurston, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert de Ufford II, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Valoignes, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr. Morì nel maggio 1370, nel Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Eva de Uffordが1305年にイギリスイギリススフォルクスウルストンで生まれた時，父Robert de Ufford IIは26歳，母Cecily de Valoignesは24歳であった。彼女はSir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Srとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1370年5月にイギリスイギリススフォルクウッドブリッジで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Eva de Ufford(이)가 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스르스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Ufford II(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Valoignes(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 우드브리지에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Eva de Ufford родилась в 1305 г. в местности турстон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Ufford II было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Valoignes, 24. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr. Она умерла май 1370 г. в местности вудбридж, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.
